{{< include /preamble/commands.qmd >}}

# Bootstrap {#sec-Bootstrap}
Bootstrapping is a resampling procedure for estimating the sampling distribution of an estimator without relying on strong distributional assumptions. Given an observed sample $x = (x_1, \dots, x_n)$, bootstrap samples are drawn with replacement (the same sample can be drawn multiple times) from $x$, each of size $n \in \N$. By computing the estimator $\hat{\theta}_b$ on many such samples, one approximates the variability of $\hat{\theta}$.

Let $\hat{\theta}$ be an estimator computed from the original sample. The bootstrap procedure can be summarized as follows:
1. Draw $B \in \N$ bootstrap samples $x_1^*, \dots, x_B^*$, each by sampling $n$ observations with replacement from $x$.
2. Compute the estimate $\hat{\theta}_b$ for each bootstrap sample $b = 1, \dots, B$.
3. Use the distribution of $\hat{\theta}_1, \dots, \hat{\theta}_B$ to estimate properties of $\hat{\theta}$.


## Bias
The bootstrap estimate of the bias of $\hat{\theta}$ is
$$
\text{Bias} = \bar{\theta} - \hat{\theta}, \quad \bar{\theta} = \frac{1}{B}\sum_{b=1}^{B}\hat{\theta}_b.
$$
This estimates how far the estimator is, on average, from the true parameter.


## Variance
The bootstrap estimate of the variance of $\hat{\theta}$ is
$$
\Var{\hat{\theta}} = \frac{1}{B - 1}\sum_{b = 1}^{B}\left(\hat{\theta}_b - \bar{\theta}\right)^2.
$$


## Confidence Intervals
A simple bootstrap confidence interval at level $1 - \alpha$ is the percentile interval, taken as the $\frac{\alpha}{2}$ and $1 - \frac{\alpha}{2}$ quantiles of $\hat{\theta}_1, \dots, \hat{\theta}_B$:
$$
\left[Q_{\alpha/2},\; Q_{1-\alpha/2}\right].
$$
